Perception of the disagreeable arises from attachment.
Perception of the disagreeable arises from attachment.
A devotee has no personal attachment to anything.
Therefore, for him, there is no question of agreeable or disagreeable.
For the service of the Lord, he can accept anything
even though it may be disagreeable to his personal interest.
In fact, he is completely free from the personal interest
and thus anything agreeable to the Lord is agreeable to him.
and thus anything agreeable to the Lord is agreeable to him.
For example, for Arjuna, at First fighting was not agreeable,
to the Lord he accepted the fighting as agreeable that is a position of a pure
devotee for his personal interest there is nothing which is agreeable or
disagreeable everything is done for the Lord and therefore he is free from
attachment and detachment that is a transcendental stage of neutrality a pure
devotee enjoys life in the pleasure of the Supreme Lord thus ends the bhaktivedanta
because of his transcendental intelligence pure devotee is
equipoised in his vision and sees himself to be uncontaminated by matter
we all spirit souls being Amsha of Krishna Krishna in the Gita says
mamaivamshu jeevaloke jeevabhoota sanatanahan
in our pure form
being amsa of the Lord, we are same in quality with the Lord.
Here it is said, he feels himself elevated to the transcendental platform of being equal in qualities with the Supreme Lord.
So in our pure stage as spirit-soul, we are same in quality with the Lord.
But because we have ichcha and dvesha,
Ichcha, dvesha, samutthena, dvandamohena, bharata.
Because we have selfish desires,
to be the bhokta, to be the enjoyer, to be the Lord,
to be the master, to be the well-wisher of other living entities.
We have selfish desires.
And Lord has facilitated this material world where we get an opportunity to fulfill these desires.
So we are not only equal in qualities, we are also equal in qualities.
Somebody asked me this question.
If Lord is all kind, Lord is all merciful,
then why has he created this world which he himself declares as dukh alayam ashashvatam.
He is all kind, he is all loving, he is our father.
Which father would like his son?
child to suffer. So why do we have a place where there is lot of misery, lot of pain,
lot of suffering, which Krishna Himself declares, it's dukhālayam aśāśvatam. Just like when
city planning happens, planning for a city happens, or a town happens, town planning
committee happens. They plan the town, okay, this is the place reserved for garden, reserved
for school, reserved for hospital, various amenities, this is a place reserved for market.
Invariably in the planning, they reserve place
for
a police station, and also some kind of a prison house, jail. Because when the planning
is done, it is understandable that some people will take law in their own hands. Now when
someone takes law in his own hands, in other words becomes a criminal, rebels against the
laws, does not want to follow the laws, creates nuisance in the society. Predominantly speaking,
government has two broad choices. One is that they can make them stand in a line and with
the AK-47, shoot them all down. But what government does is, no, being merciful, let's give them
an opportunity to reform. And While they are giving, some, they take them to a path where
some reformation
happens through punishment, through various kind of other interventions, they are so to
say, put in an area, which is called the prison
house. So, that they don't create nuisance for other law abiding citizens. And every
And every state, every country, be it a developed country, undeveloped country, there is a concept of a prison house, a jail.
Because it is expected, it is understandable that some people will rebel or will not follow the laws.
So the reason why we are in this material world is that we have Ichcha, we have Dvesha, we are envious of the Lord, we have lot of material desires.
And because of that,
Because we have various material desires, we want to gratify our senses, seek pleasure independent of the Lord through our mind and senses.
Krishna says,
We are waging a fierce battle with this material nature.
We are trying to push back.
We are trying to push back material nature, material nature which is imposing lot of restrictions, lot of misery, which we don't want.
We are trying by various ways to push back the material nature, to defeat the material nature, to win over material nature, to win over the material laws.
But Prakriti, Sthani, Karshati, we are waging a battle.
Battle means there are two forces.
So here we have material nature.
Which is imposing restrictions, birth, death, old age, disease.
And the living entity wants to push back, wants to not get defeated.
But ultimately we know, in that battle, the living entity loses.
Even if somebody is very shrewd, very this thing, you know, ultimately he has to lose the battle, he has to die.
So purity.
So what happens, oh no, if in this morning there is no..
And so eventually because of his
The beloved and devoted devotee of the Lord who is in knowledge who is full of wisdom, understands the root cause of all misery.
In fact Buddha also made this statement.
The root cause of our suffering is desires.
And
vanquish all desires, which is not the correct understanding what Bhagavatam is
giving. We cannot be desireless. The goal of human form of life is not to be like
a stone, inert, have no desires, just sit in one place doing nothing. That is not
something which is interesting, which is not possible also. Not to have a desire
is also a kind of a desire. To be desireless is a desire. So pure devotee
who is a knowledge, understands that the root cause of our suffering is material
desire.
What is material desire? Material desire is we want to satisfy our own mind and
the senses. I and mine, rooted in I and mine. My country, my family, my society, my
body, my interest, my happiness, my pleasure. That is material desire. And
spiritual desire is
desire to satisfy or please the Supreme Lord.
So understanding that root cause of our misery, our entanglement in this material
world is Iccha. Iccha is not, Iccha means material desires. Desire to enjoy
independent of Lord, desire to enjoy our own senses. A pure devotee
is completely pure of the material desires and is full of spiritual desires,
he doesn't annihilate the desires, it doesn't become that's why uh Shastra, say
AnyabhilashitaShunya,.
Shunya means zero.
It is what even Buddha said, zero.
Annihilate all desires, make all desires zero. But here it is said not just in Shunya.
AnyabhilashitaShunya.
Desire other than desires.
Desire other than desires.
desires to please the Lord. Desires other than the desires which are aligned with the Lord.
Just like in the battle of Kurukshetra here, Prabhupada is mentioning in the purport,
Arjuna had a desire not to fight. Krishna also had a desire that Arjuna should fight.
Now when Arjuna's desire got completely aligned with Krishna's desire, which is
to fight the battle. It's a spiritual desire.
Even though apparently, it looks like, what Arjuna was saying,
Arjuna was desiring, morally, ethically was very right.
Why kill unnecessarily? Why kill?
Why kill unnecessarily? Why kill?
for my own selfish motive of having a kingdom although apparently logically it looks quite
sound dynasty will be lost woman will become unchaste varna shankara will be born i will be
the cause of all this ruination for my selfish desire of getting a kingdom better for me to beg
and eat all this looks quite you know logically correct but in the second chapter krishna says
how come these impurities have come upon you so pure devotee is uncontaminated by matter he is
having pure desires and the desire is to please the supreme lord now somebody may say to serve
the supreme lord to satisfy the desires of the supreme lord is this not some kind of selfishness
or a selfish motive or a selfish motive or a selfish motive or a selfish motive or a selfish
for your own interest you're also doing bhakti for your own interest
for your emancipation for your purification it's also selfish
prabhupada was once asked swamiji are you a humanitarian humanitarian means those who are
objective is to serve humanity serve the mankind to uplift the mankind
so prabhupada said we have nothing to do with humanitarianism
so it was quite in that sense shocking somebody would hear this i mean nothing to do because it
is expected a good man is supposed to be kind should be generous should be helpful help others
so in that sense it was shocking then prabhupada explains
that we want to simply serve krishna
again it looks fanatical i have nothing to do a beggar is there in the street somebody is suffering
somebody is not having food to it nothing to do we just want to serve krishna
light some agarbatti and offer krishna
so then prabhupada explains everyone is connected to krishna is part and parcel of krishna
so pure devotee serves everyone as a devotee
everyone with the understanding that they are Krishna's. Another example is
given just like when a woman gets married she predominantly establishes a
connection with the husband and because of that one connection she automatically
gets connected to all the family members husband's brothers, husband's mother,
husband's father, brothers, sisters, all their relatives gets connected. So
likewise a pure devotee of the Lord when he wants to serve the interests of the
Lord, he wants to serve but with the right understanding, in knowledge. Prabhupada
says when a person is drowning and you save that person you get hold of his
dress and the main person is washed away by water, actually it is not
you have not really saved the person. So even Bhagavatam says yes a devotee is
very compassionate but
just intent is not enough. The intent to help others is not enough. It
has to be backed with knowledge which a pure devotee has. He is also in that sense serving the Lord.
humanity but may not be in the way which people understand as service. Sometimes
in the name of serving you can make the problem worse. For example, let's say a
person goes to visit a friend in ICU and the friend shares that you know it's so
painful you know he's going through a lot of trauma and on top of this doctors
are giving him a bland diet only boiled vegetables two times in a day and when
the person speaks all his you know difficulties now you feel compassion.
Wait a minute, wait a minute I'll get something for you and then go out and
get some
some very
very
exotic items. Now intent is right to help the person who is suffering, who is in
agony, who is not having the right food to eat but then by helping him if not
backed with proper knowledge we can do disservice to him and aggravate his
problem. This is a crude example. So in that sense a devotee is genuinely serving the
society.
He is a great humanitarian because he serves humanity with the right understanding why
the humanity is suffering. It is not that God is things are gone out of his control.
He understands why people are suffering what is the root cause of the suffering and with
that understanding what is the best solution to elevate the suffering humanity. With that,
with that knowledge he serves the humanity. And when he serves also he understands that he is
not the well wisher of the humanity. He is not Paropakaar. He is not the one who is Suridam
Sarva Bhutanam. He understands that actual Suridam Sarva Bhutanam is Krishna and he is just a mere
instrument. When he thinks like that, when he serves the Lord, when he serves humanity with
that understanding, pride doesn't touch him. He is uncontaminated by matter because he
is selflessly serving the Supreme Lord with the right understanding.
Prabhupada when he went to Mumbai, he used to travel to Pune also. There was a gentleman
by the name Mr. Malhotra.
Okay.
And whenever Mr. Malhotra, whenever Prabhupada would travel to Pune, so Mr. Malhotra would always be with Prabhupada and take him in his car wherever Prabhupada had to go.

So in that sense, he was very fortunate to spend a lot of time with Srila Prabhupada. So one time, while he was with Prabhupada, traveling in the car, going to some particular place, so Malhotraji very reluctantly asked Prabhupada, Prabhupada, can I ask you one question? I have this doubt for a long time. I was reluctant. Can I ask you?
So Prabhupada said, yes, yes, sure.
So he said, I have heard that you are building a huge, big marble temple, costly temple in Juhu, Bombay, those days, 70s, Prabhupada was building the temple in Juhu.
I have heard this, you are building a huge temple, marble temple for Krishna in Juhu, Bombay.
Is there anywhere written in Bhagavad Gita, Krishna says, build a marble temple?
Is there anywhere Krishna is demanding, build a costly temple for me?
On one side, we see there is a lot of poverty, people are dying of hunger, starvation, no proper food, no proper clothing, no proper shelter, no proper housing.
People don't have water to drink. Other side, you are building. How is it justified?
Okay, it may be justified if Krishna demands, build a costly temple for me.
He is also not asking. He is saying,
He is saying, give me one water and leaf and why build a costly temple?
So Prabhupada explained,
Just like if you go to a rich man's house and maybe in some other country,
and he is a host and you stay in his house,
and 7-10 days during your stay, the whole family was very excellent in their hospitality,
you know, took great efforts to make you comfortable, cook the best of things to serve you,
and take great care.
In other words,
it was a very, very pleasant stay.
And during that stay, you interact with the family, interact with the family members,
get to know the child, son, daughter, everyone.
And then you come back.
Let's say after a year,
you happen to see the son of that rich man in whose house you have stayed in the street.
Almost like a beggar.
Tattered clothes, no food to eat, dirty, like an urchin.
And somehow you are very sure that this is the same person whom,
son of the same rich man whom you had stayed in their house for 7-10 days.
You had interacted with this boy.
And somehow you are very sure.
So broadly speaking,
there are three options which a person can choose from.
Option number one,
it is his karma, nothing to do with me, just walk away.
Maybe some misunderstanding would have happened.
What have I to do with it? Okay.
Life will take its own course.
Just walk away.
Do nothing about it.
Do not even see in that direction.
What if he recognizes and calls you?
Why get into trouble?
Sometime it happens, accident happens, just walk away.
Otherwise, unnecessarily you will get involved.
So option number one, just do nothing,
look the other side and walk away.
Option number two,
he doesn't have proper clothes,
he doesn't have proper food,
he is in the street.
So because you are indebted for the good hospitality
they have extended during the stay,
you are obligated.
So what we do is,
go to the store,
buy the best of food,
couple of pairs of dress,
and go and give him all that.
At least 2-3 days, so that there is no shortage.
Give him some food, give him some clothes.
And have the satisfaction that,
some element of obligation is fulfilled.
Good work, what they have done,
the service they have done,
I have done my bit, small bit,
humble self, I have done.
So this is option two.
Option three is,
you go to the person,
you introduce yourself,
he recognizes you and you recognize,
ascertain that he is son of so and so person.
Double check.
After it is done,
you invite him and somehow
convince him to come with you to your home.
And in your home,
after making him comfortable,
after he takes his bath,
changes to fresh clothes,
making him comfortable,
strike a conversation
and
try to understand
as to why did he leave his house.
And the moment you stop talking,
he will start,
no, no, my father is so this thing,
this thing, that thing,
I don't want to stay with him,
he is so rash,
he scolds for small, small things,
he is very
lording tendency,
I just left my house.
All that will come up,
all that anger and hatred against the father.
So hear him out, everything,
and then he says,
looks like
there has been some misunderstanding.
Because remember when I stayed there,
your father was so kind,
the whole family is so good.
I have first hand experience of that.
Maybe what you are saying is true,
but then
looks like there is some communication gap,
some misunderstanding has crept in.
And then,
you know,
after having won his confidence,
after having heard him out,
whatever he had to share,
try to,
so to say,
the good sense in him,
prevail the good sense in him,
that how his father actually is a good father,
actually is a good person.
He is a nice person.
Just that maybe
you have misunderstood,
maybe even if he was harsh,
it was for your good.
Even if he was scolding,
maybe it is for your good.
Maybe,
could possible that
he starts looking from another perspective.
And when he is in a mood
where he is listening
and he is able to,
you know,
see things differently,
and somehow convince him,
it is not good
that you have left home.
I am sure your father also will be very disturbed.
I am sure he must be in anxiety.
He must be very pained
when he heard that you are leaving the home.
So somehow,
when the good sense prevails in him,
call the father
and tell him that your son is here.
Now you don't have to say that
I am bringing your son there.
The father will say,
wait, wait, wait.
He is so eager to meet his son.
He says,
wait, keep him there.
I am coming.
I am coming.
Next flight he will catch and come.
So of the three options,
if you analyze,
the option number three
is best because
once you connect him
with his rich father
against whom he has rebelled
and left the house
and because of which
he is in the streets
not having food to eat,
not having clothes to wear.
If he is connected with his rich father,
if he goes back home,
then the problem of feeding him,
feeding him
and giving him clothes
does not even arise.
Not for tomorrow,
not for day after tomorrow.
Permanently we have solved this problem of
food, clothing, shelter, everything.
So what a devotee is doing
is
when it comes to serving the humanity,
a lot of people misunderstand.
You are building temples,
costly temples.
What are you doing for humanity?
Even sometimes we may get a feeling
we are building huge projects,
600 crores, 700 crores.
On one side,
people are dying of hunger.
People are not having proper clothing.
How is it justified?
So we have to understand that
the Lord is all kind.
The Lord is all powerful.
I was hearing yesterday,
in one lecture,
there was one
Christian pastor.
He
was
going out for a walk,
maybe for buying something
for the church
with his small little son,
two year old baby
in a pram in that.
Unfortunately,
the place they were passing through,
there was some street fight.
Two gangs sometimes,
there is a rivalry and
they start firing.
Unfortunately,
in that street fight
between the two gangs,
notorious gangs,
they found themselves in the wrong place
at the wrong time.
And while that shooting was on,
a bullet came and hit that small little child,
two year old,
and he was killed on the spot.
So this man,
after seeing this,
he was a pastor,
preaching,
giving sermons in the church.
Two year old son,
dying right in front of his eyes,
for no fault of his,
after seeing the whole thing in front of his eyes,
he started questioning
the very foundation of fundamentals of Christianity.
The fundamentals of Christianity
is based on three principles that
God is omnipotent.
He is all powerful.
There is no power in this world
which is more powerful than God.
He is all powerful.
There is no force,
there is no power,
there is nothing which is more powerful than God.
There is nothing which is more powerful than the Lord.
This is omnipotent.
The Lord is omniscient.
He is all knowing.
He knows the past.
He knows the present.
He knows the future.
He knows who is going to fight with whom.
Who is going to shoot the bullet when.
He knows everything.
And the third principle is
He is all kind.
He is very loving.
Because he was going through trauma,
he started thinking
with what he had experienced.
His son dying.
Two year old baby.
For no fault of his.
Innocent child dying.
He started thinking
and questioning the very fundamentals of Christianity.
And he says,
it is not possible
that all three statements are true simultaneously.
One of them has to
be wrong.
So he started rationalizing.
If God is all powerful,
if we accept this statement
He is all powerful
and He is all knowing.
He knows everything.
Then maybe this statement that
He is all kind, all loving is not true.
Because
if He is all powerful,
everything was within His control.
There was no power.
Even if you call there is
Satan and there is
you know, Shaitaan
and all evil forces are there.
They somehow, sometimes
rise up.
Adharma rises, all that.
But He is all powerful.
Then He is not all kind.
Therefore you see this innocent child got killed.
For no fault of His.
Or if you think that
He is all kind.
He is all merciful.
He is very loving.
Then maybe He is not omniscient.
He is all powerful.
But maybe He is not omniscient.
He is busy in the spiritual world.
Lot of matters.
He is God.
Somehow this is God neglected.
Things went out of His control.
Or the third statement.
If He is all kind.
He is all knowing.
He knows past, present, future.
Maybe He is not all powerful.
Maybe things have gone out of His control.
He created this world with all good intentions.
Now different forces have become more powerful.
Just like now Congress has become powerful.
Suddenly some forces, some evil forces have become more powerful.
And now He has resigned.
And things have gone out of His control.
So He started very deeply questioning.
On one side we say God is all kind.
All powerful.
Omniscient.
All knowing.
All loving.
All loving.
All intelligent.
All knowing.
Then why this has happened?
Whereas Shastras, they very emphatically say that yes, God is all knowing.
Yes, God is all kind.
Yes, God is all powerful.
So Bhagavad Gita, that's why if we don't have complete knowledge of Bhagavad Gita, Bhagavatam,
which gives in complete detail of why this world is created.
What is the intent?
On our own, with our own intelligence, using our own analytical skills, we cannot comprehend,
we cannot fathom what is the intent?
What is the purpose?
Why this world is created?
Why good things are happening to bad people?
Bad things are happening to good people?
That's why seeing all this happening in this world, many people actually become atheist.
They say, where is your God?
There is no God.
Actually, the problem is not with God.
The problem is not with His creation.
The problem is that we have no proper understanding.
Why this world is created?
Why the God who is all kind, all powerful, all knowing, why there is suffering in this
world?
Is He a sadist that people are suffering and ha ha ha ha, He is enjoying?
Chalo, maro.
Accha hai.
There are people who are sadists, they get kicked when they see others suffering.
There are people like that called sadists.
When others suffer, when others are in pain, when others wriggle in pain, it gives them
enjoyment.
So is God a sadist that He has created this world where old age is there, diseases are,
death is there, and then people are suffering, and then He is there in the spiritual world,
having a good time.
So it's very bewildering.
But for a pure devotee, it's not bewildering.
He is in complete knowledge.
And because He is in knowledge, a pure devotee enjoys life and pleasure of the Supreme Lord.
He is surrendered to the Supreme Lord in knowledge.
He is completely agreeable to the Lord.
Here Prabhupada says, perception of disagreeable arises from attachment.
Because he has no material attachment, he is attached to the Lord.
He is completely in alignment with the Lord.
His desires are the desires of the Lord.
Whatever is pleasing to the Lord is pleasing to Him, even though it may be displeasing
to His mind and body.
So what we are trying to do in our practice of Krishna consciousness when we are trying
to become devotees, right now we are Go Dasa.
We are servants of our mind and body.
What is pleasing to the mind, body and senses is what we are aspiring for.
To become a devotee or a pure devotee of the Lord is, irrespective of what is pleasing
to my body and mind, I will do what is pleasing to the Lord.
Like getting up early in the morning, sadhana bhakti.
It may not be pleasing to the body and mind.
But we know that it is pleasing to, Prabhupada said that he has instituted this sadhana program.
I will do it, even though it may not be pleasing to me.
So gradually when we follow this process, the process of sadhana bhakti, purification
ensues.
And what happens when we become pure?
Then here it says, uncontaminated by matter.
Right now we are contaminated by matter.
We have attachments.
We have material desires.
And he elevates to the transcendental platform of being equal in quality.
Brahma bhuta prasannatma na shocchati na kankshati.
He becomes Brahma bhuta.
He becomes happy.
He becomes joyful.
So in that sense, Prabhupada, when he said that no, we have nothing to do with humanitarism,
it doesn't mean that a devotee is callous.
He is just interested.
Yes, I am there.
Krishna is there.
I don't care of anyone else.
He serves the humanity, serves Krishna in knowledge.
Just like I gave the example of a rich man's son.
Somebody may say, okay, you are building a temple, but then it's mainly for rich people.
You know, we see a lot of rich people coming.
What are you doing for poor people?
Actually, if a person is in knowledge, he realizes.
He realizes that.
He realizes that everyone in this material world is suffering, rich or poor.
We in illusion, we in maya think that poor man is suffering because he does not have food to eat.
He doesn't have proper place to stay.
He cannot send his son to school.
So he is suffering.
And a rich man, you see, he has a car.
He has a Bentley car.
He is living in a palatial house.
He has servants.
Therefore, he is happy.
A pure devotee who is out of maya, whose vision is perfect, who is enlightened, he sees that in this material world, everyone is suffering.
Rich people are suffering in rich conditions.
Poor people are suffering in poor conditions.
Both are suffering.
That's why Prabhupada, when he went to the West, even before he reached the US, in the Jaludutta diary, he writes,
Oh Lord, why are you taking me to a miserable place?
When we go to the West, you know, all hopes are there.
Ah, now I am going to a wonderland.
I will go to Disney World.
Full of happiness.
Full of bliss.
Prabhupada, because he was in knowledge, he is saying, why are you taking me to a miserable place?
He could see the skyscrapers.
Advance.
Big roads.
All amenities.
Spick and span infrastructure.
Why are you taking me to a miserable place?
So that is the difference between people like us and a pure devotee.
They are uncontaminated by matter.
Completely aligned with the interest of the Lord.
Anya, Bila, Ashita, Shunya.
And because they are in that stage, they are in a transcendental platform and equal in qualities with the Supreme Personality of Godhead.
Now, by following the instructions, by treading the path,
following the path which they have shown, we also have a hope that one day,
when we also become uncontaminated with all these base qualities,
we can also experience what a pure devotee experiences.
Directly in company of the Lord.
Enjoying the bliss of, here it says, a devotee enjoys life in the pleasure of the Supreme Lord.
Otherwise, we are like beggars.
Like a beggar, you know, just begging for alms.
Some broken pieces of some chapatis.
Likewise, we are like beggars.
Begging for happiness.
And what we get is small, small tits and bits here and there.
In contrast, what we are eligible for, what we are entitled for,
what we can actually experience is a constant bliss in association with the Supreme Lord.
Because of our own material desires, because of our own material attachments,
we have got disconnected from the Lord.
And the process of sadhana bhakti which Srila Prabhupada is giving us
is helping us to connect back to our rich father.
Srila Prabhupada ki grantharaj simad bhagavatam ki.
